item.title: Bush vasectomy
item.description: A cautionary tale for those who regularly go bush. This photo is from one of my colleagues up north. The driver of this Nissan Patrol drove over a branch on the road while on field work in a bush area. The branch broke and flicked up under the vehicle and hit the floor pan. It then slid along the metal floor pan until it got to the plastic drain plug, the only weak spot. This is the result. Luckily, there was no passenger on board or he would have likely required hospital treatment for a bush vasectomy. Never drive over large fallen branches. Either stop and remove the obstacle, or drive around it. There's a lot at stake. Here endeth the lesson.
item.dc:creator: OzRockabella

item.title: Gang of smols appear out of nowhere and attack
item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: Wednesday wholesome dump to help you finish the week : )
item.description:
1. Silla the cat wants to learn : )
2. She is all grown up
3. Cute corgi boi going for a ride with cute little hoodie
4. Can I?
5. PS1 with PS1 graphics. There is something so wholesome and relaxing about this.
6. The way this corgi is excited about lettuce
7. Love when I meet people like this :)
8. The proper way to eat cotton candy (she's a keeper, for sure)
9. Swimming Reflexes
10
11. That coordination
12. There's something wrong with that boah
13. Kid uses toy fishing rod and actually catches a fish
14. You've been scrolling too much, gotta slap you with a double dog tax. Sorry
15. Thanks for scrolling all the way trough! Hope this dump made you happy! Finally I would like to share with you a little passion project of mine. Me and my brother are making a game that is inspired by "The Stanley Parable" and the narrator definitely has a touch a of GladOS to him. The sincere request is if you like what you see on Steam to please wishlist, it's the best way to help us during development :) https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ P.S. HAVE A GREAT REST OF THE WEEK!

item.title: The Muse
item.description: This comic was published a while back on Patreon. Our supporters regularly get insights into our sketchbooks and have access to strips that nobody else gets to see. They loved this story so much that they encouraged us to publish a full color version. So here it is! Cheers to our patrons – you’re the moose we love to kiss! https://www.patreon.com/warandpeas

item.title: THE CHANGE WE NEED.
item.description: Americans buy 29 BILLION water bottles a year. For every six bottles people buy, only one is recycled . That leads to a big problem given the fact that water bottles do not biodegrade, but rather photodegrade. This means that it takes at least up to 1,000 years for every single bottle to decompose, leaking pollutants into our soil and water and microplastics back into the food chain, every day. Yuck! If you even thought about buying a bottle of water 30 years ago, you would be looked at like you needed a rubber room, yet here we are. It is estimated that 14% of littered waste actually stem from beverage containers and 8 million tons of plastic bottles enter our oceans every year.
item.dc:creator: doxydad1

item.dc:creator: awesomeoldguy

item.title: A religious joke
item.description: Now this is just a little humor so no one get their boxers in a bunch: Four Catholic men and a Catholic woman were having coffee. The first Catholic man tells his friends, "My son is a priest, when he walks into a room, everyone calls him 'Father'." The second Catholic man chirps, "My son is a Bishop. When he walks into a room people call him 'Your Grace'." The third Catholic gent says, "My son is a Cardinal.. When he enters a room everyone says 'Your Eminence'." The fourth Catholic man then says, "My son is the Pope. When he walks into a room people call him 'Your Holiness'." Since the lone Catholic woman was sipping her coffee in silence, the four men give her a subtle, "Well....?" She proudly replies, "I have a daughter, slim, tall, 38D breast, 24" waist and 34" hips. When she walks into a room, people say, "Oh My God."
item.dc:creator: ssumner13

item.title: When you absolutely positively want to scare the [bleep] out of whomever is reading your sign
item.description:
Because “Loose lips sink ships” wasn’t quite emphatic enough
Aerated water is fucking scary. It's water that has a fuckton of gas in it, which reduces the buoyancy to the point where you will immediately start to sink if you fall in.
https://www.tumblr.com/neil-gaiman/748612960397803520?source=share
RISK OF CRUSHING WARNING Prohibited to transport goods in lifts that do not have an inner door
https://www.boredpanda.com/scary-creepy-signs/
https://www.ranker.com/list/best-scary-signs/miguelgr
https://www.ranker.com/list/scarysigns/samicalderon
tax

item.title: MRW I find a way to bypass the broken imgur upload size limit and can once again pack in every last pixel to destroy the servers.
item.description: Source: Deadpool and Wolverine #intergalacticqualitygifs (Check WiFi) Imgur, TAKE NOTE this does not give you any right to ignore the glaring upload problem. And though I DID find a work-a-round, you are still noticeably degrading the quality. The community really needs to voice their objections to all this or nothing will change. Edit: So something has hit/stalled this post. I'm just trying to help make things better and bring attention to our needs as the content creators and driving forces behind this awesome site.
item.dc:creator: BabyFarkMcGeeZax

item.title: SecretHarpy's Meme Library: Volume 26
item.description:
Hello and welcome to another meme dump, my imgur friends. My brain is too fuzzy for an opening witticism, so...onward, to the funny stuff!
I wish it were that simple. When I was training the instructors were very clear. "When you're starting compressions, if it doesn't sound like you're crushing a bag of potato chips, you're not doing it hard enough." Sorry in advance to anyone I may use this skill on but...I guess a bunch of broken ribs is better than being dead? ....aaanyway, let's move on from that fun visual
woah mama
reeeERRRReeeERRR KSSSSHKKK kssshHHHHHHKKKK
ahahahaAHAHAHAHAHAHA
That's a dream job right there. Dark, quiet, no fucking people around, just able to do my thing while chilling with the ghosts? Hell yeah sign me up
Still gonna be falling off rainbow road but I'm okay with this
Like the devil's armpit over here. I hate it.
Yeah no thanks
Also Operation, my first experience getting jumpscared like that
That's all for this one folks. I hope it brought you some joy. Until next time my imgur friends, stay awesome.
item.dc:creator: SecretHarpy

item.dc:creator: Pasquinel

item.title: Would the money have been nice yeah, but this feels right.
item.description: I bought the ticket on my way to work on a whim, I never do it but what the hell it's my friday, so before work I scratched it and won. Later I heard that Larry the material handler has been having to take the bus to work cause his car broke down. I like Larry he's a good natured, funny guy who busts his ass and never complains. They don't make a lot of money and this 1,000 wasn't going to make or break me. And god dammit he couldn't believe it and was gobsmacked someone would do this for him and that was all the thanks I needed.
item.dc:creator: Iblamemyparentstoo

item.title: Time for another unhinged XL Wednesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
no way they were supposed to look like anything else
i've had one in the freezer for at least 4 days
i should probably watch this show
2nd snack
don't you hate pants?
not sure what that might entail
found it
finally it's here!
i guess
obviously
mmm hmm
"i am a generous god"
not very appetizing and i like shrimp
every year for me
i'm ok with the size of my peehole
this scene in the movie (basic instinct) was kind of legendary back in my day for... reasons
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Images of Note: Chonky
item.description:
And the piss meme continues
Wizard Bicycle!
Truly satanic
:)
Mood
I should install the Stardew Valley Polyamory Mod in my next run through.
1798 was a wild year
Yeah, same in the UK :(
Yeah
B O M K
That...that isn't...
Shane when you give him beer at any time
NUKE AT THE CLUB
(smol)
lol
C H O N K Y
I feel bad for him
GUP
They know
pea
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA
Yes this is a good way
the horrors
They geese know only violence
Yeah
The memes are going to be fire.
it worked and now they have proof of what they did that can be used in a court of law.
They're either going to fail miserably or water the lore down to the point that it sucks.
Jesus christ Nickelodeon.
For Chats: http://discord.abbii.gay For Shitposts: http://tumblr.abbii.gay For srs and maybe shitposts: http://mastodon.abbii.gay Also STREEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEAM: http://twitch.abbii.gay

item.title: I wear the (largest possible) cone of shame.
item.description:
I could sit and list all the variables that led me to believe this was a legit request to verify my bank details. Like how experienced Etsy sellers (via YouTube) explained how easy it was to get your shop suspended early on — and how it's more likely if you use a smaller bank, like I do. Or how etsy's *actual* sales portal for listings has been buggy from the start, with completely non-functional Preview buttons and no ability to Save Draft for new listings. (Literally had to publish unfinished listings in order to preview/save them, then promptly deactivate the listing to keep working on it in an unpublished state.) But mostly...
The biggest issue is not the $800 — my bank, though small, is good. The fraud reporting process means I won't be on the hook for that... presumably. The big issue is replacing that card. I won't go into details, but I had to replace another card a couple months ago and it was a massive hassle. (That one was the not-my-fault kind of fraud... probably....? Ugh.) Here's how to be an idiot:
See "ShopHelper" and — because you're prepared for some sort of message from Etsy about verifying your small bank... assume it's legit. Double check the From email is Etsy's legit email — actually search your email for past messages and confirm. Yep. But then *completely* neglect the weird used name (b488puj5xuuerzaj) and the oddness of my newest listing linked in the message... And click "View message."
Here's the next glaring idiot move: open the PDF. And *completely ignore* the warning from actual-Etsy: "To stay protected, stay on Etsy. Never follow links to other sites, and don't share contact, account, or financial info with anyone in Messages. Learn how to spot spam." What excuses can I make? That I see so many little notifications and popups everywhere on the Internet that they now blur into the background and I don't really see them anymore, so Etsy's warning was invisible to me? That I was *expecting* a message from Etsy about verifying my account info, so I didn't look closely when one showed up? That I'm a moron?
The first sentences disabled whatever was left of my typical "is this a scam" radar... because I was expecting to be suspended. I'd been told (by a YouTuber with a respectable 300k following and about a hundred legit videos describing how to navigate selling on Etsy) that sellers using small banks get suspended and it's normal. I'd been told you can get suspended for several "they're just trying to stop scammers" reasons, and all you have to do is verify/update your info and you're good. If I'd been thinking at all, then I would have never opened the PDF (so stupid) and I would have never scanned the goddam QR code. (And I'd have noticed the typo at the end of the message, but we're way past that now.)
...Yep. I did. Because I was expecting to be asked for my financial info, I input it without hesitation. (Yes, I know this was stupid.) ((So painfully stupid.))
Once I input my info, a "Support Chat" opened up. And by warning me that scammers would try to get my info, they effectively made themselves seem like blue team, rather than red team. I fell for it. Don't do what I did. ...Which is why I'm sharing all this. Admitting my idiocy with the hope that some other Etsy user who sees this doesn't make the same mistake (after mistake after mistake) that I made.
Like watching a goddam train wreck.
By the time I figured it out, I'd already done all they needed me to.
A waking nightmare to realize what had just happened. To realize that despite all my many warnings to friends and family over the years — sending them examples of phishing messages so they'd know what to watch out for... That despite *fully knowing better* and knowing what phishing looks like... I fell for it. I was expecting to be asked for this info, so it didn't raise more than a moment of concern when I was asked. And to top it all off: Yes, the SMS messages from my bank did say to never share the code with anyone. My only excuse for why I did is that I'm (an idiot, and I'm) unfamiliar with the new systems I'm using — and I guess it made me naïve to the potential for being scammed. I let my guard down. Extensively.
Sigh.
He said they were based in Ukraine — likely Russian attackers. And he said even the savviest of tech-savvy can fall for phishing scams. I don't accept his efforts to comfort me — I could have prevented all this by doing a dozen things differently. Too many mistakes that I knew better than to make. A constellation of variables made me supremely gullible. Indefensibly so.
Please be nice. I know I screwed up. What a welcome wagon to Etsy, I guess.
item.dc:creator: lelindepage
